title: advance_diversifier_to monotonicity guard in a transaction prevents address reuse
description: advance_diversifier_to wraps the read-then-write in a SQLite transaction and checks if idx <= current before writing. A contributor may suggest removing the transaction ('max_connections=1 makes it safe') or removing the guard ('callers should be responsible'). The transaction makes the intent explicit and survives pool reconfiguration. The guard prevents a stale retry from rolling back the stored index after Sapling skipped an invalid diversifier — the exact scenario that causes address reuse. The advance_diversifier_to_is_monotonic and advance_diversifier_to_corrupt_value_returns_err tests verify both properties.
